parts.text:
Subject: Local Childcare Funding Measure (Proposition [Number/Name])

Hi [Neighbor's Name],

I hope you’ve been doing well and getting some decent sleep between your shifts! I can only imagine how tough it is juggling work and being a solo dad with a night-shift schedule.

I’m reaching out because I’ve been looking into the new childcare funding measure on the upcoming ballot, and I wanted to share some concerns I have about how it might impact neighbors in your specific position.

While the measure is being pitched as a way to help parents, I’m worried it doesn’t actually account for the reality of non-traditional work hours. Most of the programs this tax would fund are for standard 9-to-5 daycare centers. Since you’re working nights and likely need very different types of support or flexibility, it feels like this measure completely overlooks the needs of parents who don't work a typical office schedule.

Furthermore, the funding for this comes from [mention specific tax, e.g., property tax increases or a new local levy]. At a time when the cost of living is already so high, I hate the idea of seeing your taxes go up to fund a system that doesn't actually provide a service you can use. It feels like you’d be paying out of pocket to subsidize a system that stays closed while you’re at work.

I think we deserve a more inclusive plan that supports all types of families and work schedules without just adding another tax burden on single parents. I’m planning to vote "No" on this particular measure for those reasons, and I wanted to share that perspective in case you hadn't had a chance to dive into the fine print yet.

Anyway, hope to catch you outside soon when the weather's nice. Take care!

Best,

[Your Name]
